title: Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision
summary: A conservative stop rule for worsening snow, wind, cold, road closure, or lost visibility that prioritizes shelter before navigation becomes impossible.
content:
A conservative stop rule for worsening snow, wind, cold, road closure, or lost visibility that prioritizes shelter before navigation becomes impossible.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: vehicle winter kit or walking shelter kit
steps: Cancel or stop travel when official closures, rapidly falling visibility, drifting snow, unsafe traction, or loss of landmarks appears.
Choose the nearest verified heated shelter or safe parked location before fuel, battery, daylight, or body heat becomes critical.
Share location and plan with one contact; if stranded in a vehicle, remain with it unless a visible occupied shelter is very close and safely reachable.
Prevent carbon-monoxide exposure by keeping exhaust clear, ventilating appropriately, and never running combustion heaters in enclosed spaces.
warnings: Do not follow an unverified shortcut in whiteout conditions.
Shivering, confusion, clumsiness, or unusual sleepiness are medical warning signs.
common_mistakes: Starting Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Blizzard or whiteout travel-stop decision.']
